name: Chungcheongnam-do (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the future of the province
the Yellow Sea coast and Geum River, evoking openness and progress

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised multicolour emblem combining curved forms and the wordmark 'Chungcheongnam-do'.
history: South Chungcheong Province (Chungcheongnam-do), facing the Yellow Sea on Korea's west coast, has used several official symbols. The current provincial flag and emblem date from a 2010s redesign that uses curved abstract motifs to represent the Yellow Sea coast, the Geum River, and the dynamic future of the province following the partial relocation of national administrative functions to Sejong (which was carved out of South Chungcheong in 2012).

features.symbolism: abstract multicolour motif evoking the Yellow Sea coast, the Geum River, and the floral symbols of the province; expresses dynamism and harmony between nature and people
